name: SMART Grid Full
description: The 'SMART Grid Full' conspiracy theory posits that the global implementation of smart grids – advanced electrical grids using digital technology to improve efficiency and reliability – is a covert operation with ulterior motives beyond energy management. Proponents claim the technology is being used for mass surveillance, mind control, and population control. They fear that smart meters, a key component of smart grids, constantly monitor energy usage and transmit data that can be used to track individuals' activities inside their homes, violating privacy. Further, some believe the radio frequency (RF) radiation emitted by these devices causes health problems, despite scientific consensus generally finding the levels to be safe. A more extreme version of the theory suggests the grid is being weaponized, capable of inducing illness or even death through directed energy. The origin of the theory can be traced back to early concerns surrounding data privacy and EMF sensitivity, amplified by online forums and alternative media outlets. Evidence cited includes anecdotal reports of health issues after smart meter installation, misinterpreted scientific studies on EMF radiation, and concerns about government surveillance capabilities. Mainstream perspectives view smart grids as a crucial step towards sustainable energy management. They emphasize that data collected is anonymized and used for grid optimization, not individual tracking. Regulatory bodies set standards for data privacy and EMF emissions, and scientific studies consistently show no significant health risks from smart meter radiation at typical exposure levels. Critics point out the lack of credible evidence to support claims of mind control or weaponization, attributing the theory to misinformation and fear-mongering.
subtitle: Smart grids as a tool for control?

claims.claim: Smart grids are used for mass surveillance.
claims.evidence: Data collection from smart meters reveals personal habits and activities.
claims.criticism: Data is anonymized and aggregated for grid optimization, not individual tracking. Privacy regulations are in place.

claims.claim: Smart meter radiation causes health problems.
claims.evidence: Anecdotal reports of illness after smart meter installation; misinterpreted studies on EMF radiation.
claims.criticism: Scientific consensus indicates that smart meter radiation levels are safe and pose no significant health risks.

claims.claim: The smart grid is a weapon for population control.
claims.evidence: Speculation about directed energy weapons and the grid's potential for manipulation.
claims.criticism: Lack of credible evidence. Claims are based on speculation and misinformation.

keyFigures.name: Deanna Spingola
keyFigures.role: Author and promoter of smart grid conspiracy theories

keyFigures.name: David Robert Grimes
keyFigures.role: Physicist and science communicator, debunks health claims related to EMF

mythologyConnections: Technological Singularity (fear of technology surpassing humanity)

timeline.event: Initial concerns about data privacy and EMF sensitivity emerge.
American Recovery and Reinvestment Act funds smart grid projects, sparking conspiracy theories.
Continued spread of misinformation and resistance to smart meter installations.

sources.author: Arthur Firstenberg
sources.work: The Invisible Rainbow: A History of Electricity and Life
sources.title: Arthur Firstenberg

sources.author: National Institute of Environmental Health Sciences (NIEHS)
sources.work: EMF Electric and Magnetic Fields Associated With the Use of Electric Power
sources.title: National Institute of Environmental Health Sciences (NIEHS)
